title: Support One-to-Many Logic Definitions per Metric
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to associate multiple logic implementations with a single metric, so that I can track how the same metric is expressed across different systems (Salesforce, Snowflake, Tableau, etc.).
description:
Refactor the metric logic relationship from a single text field to a one-to-many model. A single metric can be implemented across multiple systems with different expressions (SQL, Python, Tableau formulas, etc.).

### Use Cases
- Same metric implemented in two Snowflake orgs for different divisions
- A metric implemented in both Salesforce and Snowflake with different logic
- Logic expressed as SQL, Python, and Tableau expressions for the same metric

### Goal
Maintain both the logical definition and the mapping/lineage to where logic has been implemented across systems. This preserves semantic metadata from multiple sources.
acceptance_criteria: Multiple logic implementations can be associated with a single metric
Each logic entry supports a system-of-origin identifier
UI allows viewing and managing multiple logic entries per metric
Existing single-logic metrics are migrated to the new model
ux_changes: UI updated to display and manage multiple logic entries per metric.
middleware_changes: Refactored logic storage from single text field to one-to-many relationship.
data_model_changes: New one-to-many relationship table for metric logic implementations with system-of-origin metadata.
implementation_notes: Supports cross-system metric lineage tracking. Logic can be expressed in different languages (SQL, Python, Tableau expressions).
comment_summary: Waqar updated the logic to support one-to-many relationships and updated the UI on development. Shared a screenshot for review.
